name: endpoint_excludes_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_endpoint_excludes_zero(blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
    ensures
        blocking_endpoint_index > 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, endpoint index 0 is never valid, silently excluding the first descriptor slot

name: requires_vacuous_on_index
code:
proof fn phi_2_requires_vacuous_on_index(blocking_endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        0 <= blocking_endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If entailed, MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS is 0 and no valid index exists, making the syscall unreachable

name: error_indistinguishable_from_success
reason: If entailed, the caller cannot distinguish a failed receive from a successful one

name: scheduler_capacity_zero
reason: If entailed, the scheduler-full check always triggers and the syscall can never successfully schedule a blocked sender

name: queue_capacity_zero
reason: If entailed, the endpoint queue capacity is zero so the queue-length branch conditions degenerate and no sender can ever be queued
